Although the denoising network predicts the added noise
ϵk, directly learning the full noise can be suboptimal due
to its inherent stochasticity. To address this, we introduce a
residual refinement module that explicitly learns to correct
the residual error ∆ϵk not captured by the main diffusion
model. The final refined noise is computed as:
ϵrefined,k = ϵk + ∆ϵk, (13)
where ∆ϵk = RefineNet(G′
o, G′
s, G′
l, ϵk, Ek) ∈ Rtobs×2 de-
notes the predicted residual by the refinement network.
Training Optimization
To train the proposed method, we incorporate the short-term
loss and the long-term loss in addition to the diffusion loss.
The short-term loss Ls supervises the intention estima-
tor to more accurately approximate pedestrian motion ten-
dencies within the observation context. Given the polar rep-
resentation (θ, r) of intention, the angular component is
trained using cosine distance to address its circular nature,
while the magnitude is optimized via mean squared error.

The long-term loss guides the endpoint estimator to cap-
ture global motion patterns and accurately predict pedestrian
destinations. Rather than optimizing over all candidate end-
